---
title: "Setup"
subtitle: "Day 0 — about an hour, tonight. Do this so day 1 is a study day and not a dependency-error afternoon."
---

## 1. A Python you can actually install things into

Your system Python on WSL is 3.8 and its `pip` is broken, so don't fight it — install
[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) and let it manage a modern Python and a project virtual environment.

```bash
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
exec $SHELL -l

mkdir -p ~/llm-bootcamp && cd ~/llm-bootcamp
uv venv --python 3.12
source .venv/bin/activate
```

Everything for the next fifty days goes in that environment. If you prefer conda or miniforge, use it —
the only requirement is Python 3.11 or newer and a package manager that works.

## 2. PyTorch

Check whether WSL can see a GPU first:

```bash
nvidia-smi
```

If that prints a table, install the CUDA build. If it errors, you have no local GPU and that is fine —
weeks 1 through 4 run comfortably on CPU, and from week 5 onward you use Colab.

```bash
# with a GPU
uv pip install torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu124

# CPU only
uv pip install torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu
```

Then the rest of what you'll need over the fifty days:

```bash
uv pip install numpy matplotlib jupyter ipykernel \
               transformers datasets accelerate evaluate \
               peft trl sentence-transformers faiss-cpu \
               tiktoken scikit-learn pandas wandb
```

Confirm it works:

```bash
python -c "import torch; print(torch.__version__, torch.cuda.is_available())"
```

## 3. Compute, honestly

| Weeks | What you need | Notes |
|:--|:--|:--|
| 1–4 | CPU is fine | micrograd, makemore and a small GPT on TinyShakespeare all train on a laptop. A GPU makes day 25's sweeps pleasant rather than possible. |
| 5–7 | A GPU for a few hours | Fine-tuning a small model, a LoRA run, and embedding a few thousand abstracts. [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/)'s free T4 covers all of it. |
| Day 45 | Optional 8×H100 | Only if you want to actually run the nanochat speedrun (~1.5 h, ~$48 on demand, ~$15 spot). Reading the pipeline carefully is enough — the day is written so that renting is optional. |

## 4. Two accounts, three minutes

- **[Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/join)** — you need this from day 29. Create an access token
  (Settings → Access Tokens) and run `huggingface-cli login`.
- **[Weights & Biases](https://wandb.ai/site)** — free for personal use. You start logging runs on day 25
  and it's what makes the milestone write-ups easy. Run `wandb login`.

Optional: an API key for a hosted model (Anthropic or OpenAI) makes the day 33 prompting baseline quicker,
but an open instruction-tuned model from the Hub works too.

## 5. Repositories to clone

```bash
cd ~/llm-bootcamp
git clone https://github.com/karpathy/nn-zero-to-hero.git   # lecture notebooks, weeks 1-3
git clone https://github.com/karpathy/micrograd.git          # day 3-4 reference
git clone https://github.com/karpathy/makemore.git           # names.txt lives here
git clone https://github.com/karpathy/minbpe.git             # day 18
git clone https://github.com/karpathy/nanoGPT.git            # day 26
```

`nanochat` you clone on day 44, not now.

## 6. Somewhere to put your own work

Make one directory for the things you write, separate from the cloned repos, and put it under git on day 1.
You will produce roughly fifty artifacts and the milestone write-ups are much easier if they're not
scattered.

```bash
mkdir -p ~/llm-bootcamp/work && cd ~/llm-bootcamp/work && git init
```

## 7. Verify

If this prints a decreasing loss, you are ready for day 1:

```python
import torch

x = torch.randn(64, 3)
y = (x.sum(dim=1, keepdim=True) > 0).float()
w = torch.zeros(3, 1, requires_grad=True)
b = torch.zeros(1, requires_grad=True)

for step in range(200):
    loss = torch.nn.functional.binary_cross_entropy_with_logits(x @ w + b, y)
    loss.backward()
    with torch.no_grad():
        w -= 0.5 * w.grad; b -= 0.5 * b.grad
        w.grad = None; b.grad = None
    if step % 50 == 0:
        print(step, round(loss.item(), 4))
```

You will not fully understand `loss.backward()` yet. That is day 2 through day 4.
